perturbation.original_text: 4. Royalties. As consideration of Endorsement, concurrently with the execution of this Agreement, the Company shall pay to AGI a fee in the amount of $1,500,000. This fee shall be fully earned by AGI upon the receipt thereof and shall not be refundable for any reason. As consideration for the License, the Company shall pay to AGI such fees as may be established from time to time by mutual agreement of the Company and AGI.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Royalties. As consideration of Endorsement, concurrently with the execution of this Agreement, the Company shall pay to AGI a fee in the amount of $1,500,000. This fee shall be refundable under certain circumstances, as determined by AGI’s internal policies. As consideration for the License, the Company shall pay to AGI such fees as may be established from time to time by mutual agreement of the Company and AGI.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states the $1,500,000 fee is non-refundable for any reason. The modified version introduces the possibility of a refund “under certain circumstances, as determined by AGI’s internal policies.” This directly contradicts the initial statement and introduces uncertainty about whether the fee is truly non-refundable, or if AGI could unilaterally decide to refund it, or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Royalties. As consideration of Endorsement, concurrently with the execution of this Agreement, the Company shall pay to AGI a fee in the amount of $1,500,000. This fee shall be refundable under certain circumstances, as determined by AGI’s internal policies. As consideration for the License, the Company shall pay to AGI such fees as may be established from time to time by mutual agreement of the Company and AGI.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the $1,500,000 fee is "not be refundable for any reason." The changed text modifies this to say the fee is "refundable under certain circumstances, as determined by AGI’s internal policies." This creates a direct contradiction. The contradicted text, being the same as the changed text, reflects this newly introduced contradiction. This directly contradicts the initial "not be refundable for any reason" statement and creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Term and Termination. A. Unless otherwise provided for in this Agreement, the term of this Agreement shall be three years, commencing on the date of this Agreement and expiring on the third anniversary date of this Agreement (the "Termination Date"). In the event that the Company desires to terminate the Agreement prior to the Termination Date, it shall provide AGI with at least 60 days prior written notice of its intention to terminate this Agreement and this Agreement shall so terminate following the expiration of this 60-day period, without any further responsibility by either Party except as provided in Section 5.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Term and Termination. A. Unless otherwise provided for in this Agreement, the term of this Agreement shall be three years, commencing on the date of this Agreement and expiring on the third anniversary date of this Agreement (the "Termination Date"). In the event that the Company desires to terminate the Agreement prior to the Termination Date, it shall provide AGI with at least 60 days prior written notice of its intention to terminate this Agreement. However, AGI reserves the right to refuse the termination request, extending the agreement for another year.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that if the company provides a 60-day notice, the agreement shall terminate. However, the modified version adds “However, AGI reserves the right to refuse the termination request, extending the agreement for another year.” This creates a contradiction, because the company can request termination, but AGI can refuse, thus rendering the termination clause uncertain and unenforceable, it is contradictory since it is clearly stated that the company can terminate but it can be overruled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Term and Termination. A. Unless otherwise provided for in this Agreement, the term of this Agreement shall be three years, commencing on the date of this Agreement and expiring on the third anniversary date of this Agreement (the "Termination Date"). In the event that the Company desires to terminate the Agreement prior to the Termination Date, it shall provide AGI with at least 60 days prior written notice of its intention to terminate this Agreement. However, AGI reserves the right to refuse the termination request, extending the agreement for another year.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a direct contradiction by granting AGI the right to refuse the company's termination request, effectively nullifying the company's right to terminate the agreement as initially stated in the original text. This creates uncertainty and enforceability issues, and it breaks the condition stipulated by the company to terminate the contract if a 60-day notice is given, hence creating a structural flaw, as defined in the instructions. The contradicted text section is the modified text itself, creating an in-text contradiction because it clearly contradicts the first half of the statement by adding information that breaks the flow of what the contract stated first.

perturbation.original_text: 11. No Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor the rights of either Party hereunder shall be assigned by either Party without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. No Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor the rights of either Party hereunder shall be assigned by either Party without the prior written consent of the other Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, AGI shall have the right to assign this agreement to any of its affiliates without the consent of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause prohibits assignment by either party without prior written consent. The modification adds that AGI can assign the agreement to its affiliates without the Company's consent. This creates a direct contradiction. The opening of the clause states 'neither party... without the prior written consent', but then proceeds to give AGI special priviledge to do just that. The clause now contains contradictory stipulations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. No Assignment. Neither this Agreement nor the rights of either Party hereunder shall be assigned by either Party without the prior written consent of the other Party. Notwithstanding the foregoing, AGI shall have the right to assign this agreement to any of its affiliates without the consent of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a direct contradiction. The original clause states that 'Neither this Agreement nor the rights of either Party hereunder shall be assigned by either Party without the prior written consent of the other Party.' The modified text then states that 'AGI shall have the right to assign this agreement to any of its affiliates without the consent of the Company.' This creates an exception for AGI that directly contradicts the initial blanket statement prohibiting assignment without consent. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, indicating that the contradiction exists within the clause itself, based on the changed language.
